name: Frankie Carle
abstract: Frankie Carle (born Francis Nunzio Carlone, March 25, 1903 – March 7, 2001) was an American pianist and bandleader. As a very popular bandleader in the 1940s and 1950s, Carle was nicknamed "The Wizard of the Keyboard" for his piano skills.

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: Absolutely. Really that was how I learned how to play. I liked his solos. I liked his piano playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: In the meantime I had to make some money so I joined Frankie Carle’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Oh yes, Franklie Carle on piano, and at one time when he made a comeback, he had Frankie Carle, and he had Gene Krupa with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: Tony Ginsanti, he went with Horace Hite, and then with Frankie Carle, when Frankie Carle left Horace Heidt, he went with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Strazzeri
about_quotes.evidence: he used to tell me, why do you play like Frankie Carle, where I could hear the melody
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Strazzeri interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: the original premise of the show was, I think, his vocals and the band of Frankie Carl, the pianist. ... we wound up on the show
